commentary_text: The market assigns Germany a 55–57% win probability versus the model's 35%, a 20–22 percentage-point gap favoring the favourite. The model sees this as a nearly toss-up three-way contest; the market sees a clear German edge. This gap could reflect recent form, injury status, or lineup changes that the model's Elo ratings do not yet capture. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth monitoring—either the market has fresher information, or the model's longer historical baseline will prove more predictive. Neither interpretation is settled.
